item.title: Playwrights launch platform Pandulipi Pathmancha
item.description: The Pandulipi Pathmancha, an organisation for playwrights, began its journey on Saturday featuring a script reading by renowned playwright, director, and actor Mamunur Rashid at the Bangladesh Shilpakala Academy’s seminar hall....

item.title: Gallery MAFA inaugurates 5-day ‘Winter Light on Canvas’
item.description: The Mostafiz Academy of Fine Arts inaugurated a five-day group art exhibition titled Winter Light on Canvas at Gallery MAFA of Lalmatia in Dhaka on Friday....

item.title: NU to plant 2cr trees in 5yrs: VC
item.description: National University has taken an initiative to tackle climate change risks and build eco-friendly green campuses....

item.title: Woman, 3 daughters killed in Lakshmipur laid to rest in Cumilla
item.description: A woman and her three daughters who were hacked to death in Lakhsmipur on Thursday were laid to rest at the family graveyard of Homna in Cumilla on Friday night....

item.title: Locals besiege power office over outages in Sirajganj
item.description: Residents of Raiganj in Sirajganj on Saturday besieged a local rural electricity office and staged a demonstration in protest against prolonged power outages and severe load-shedding....

item.title: Bill tabled for fast-track drug trial
item.description: Home minister Salahuddin Ahmed on Saturday tabled the Narcotics Control (Amendment) Bill, 2026 in Jatiya Sangsad, aimed at strengthening narcotics control through introducing specialised tribunals to ensure the speedy trial of drug-related offences....

item.title: JU approves Tk 348cr budget, student reps rejoin senate after 34yrs
item.description: Jahangirnagar University approved a Tk 348.70 crore budget for the 2026-27 financial year on Saturday, as student representatives took part in the university’s Senate for the first time in nearly 34 years, marking the restoration of student participation in the institution’s highest policy-making body....

item.title: Female doctor found dead at Shahbagh flat
item.description: The police suspected that the female doctor, whose body was recovered at an apartment of the Aziz Super Market building at Shahbagh in the capital on Friday, had been dead for days....

item.title: Developers fear housing sector slowdown
item.description: Real estate developers in Rajshahi on Saturday expressed concern over increases in taxes in the housing sector in the proposed national budget for the 2026-2027 fiscal year and warned that the measures could discourage investment, reduce housing projects......

item.title: Juba League leader hacked to death in Feni
item.description: A Juba League leader was hacked to death in the Nijkunjara Samity Bazar area of Ghopal Union under Chhagalnaiya upazila in Feni on Saturday....

item.title: 3 Bangladeshi expats of a family stabbed to death in Rome
item.description: Three members of a Bangladeshi expatriate family in Italy have been murdered in a knife attack on Friday....

item.title: Shahe Alam rejects ‘Bogura first’ allegation
item.description: The state minister for local government, rural development and cooperatives, Mir Shahe Alam, on Saturday rejected allegations that Bogura had received preferential treatment in development allocations, saying that official figures showed the district ranked......

item.title: BB ends CAMELS ratings for banks
item.description: The Bangladesh Bank has discontinued the CAMELS rating system for banks, replacing it with a forward-looking Composite Risk Rating (CRR) framework under its new Risk-Based Supervision (RBS) architecture......

item.title: Smartphone prices may rise as tax concession expires by June
item.description: Prices of imported smartphones might increase from July 1 as a temporary government concession on import duty is set to expire on June 30, while the proposed budget for the 2026–27 financial year contained no provision to extend the facility......

item.title: The saga of four Indians pushed into Bangladesh
item.description: Four Indian nationals, including two children, have remained stuck in Bangladesh for nearly a year after being pushed across the border by India’s Border Security Force. ...

item.title: EV sector faces infrastructure, energy crisis
item.description: Energy crisis, inadequate charging infrastructure and high price remain major barriers to electric vehicle adoption in Bangladesh, said industrialists and experts on Saturday......

item.title: Cybersecurity now central to national security: Swapon
item.description: Information and broadcasting minister Zahir Uddin Swapon on Saturday said that cybersecurity had become a central pillar of national security, stressing the need for Bangladesh to accelerate information technology adoption and develop a skilled workforce to keep pace with rapid global technological change......

item.title: BGB foils 2 more push-in bids in Habiganj, Meherpur
item.description: The Border Guard Bangladesh and local people foiled two more push-in attempts by the Indian Border Security Force through several points in Habiganj and Meherpur districts between Friday and Saturday....

item.title: Social business practitioners urged to turn ideas into action
item.description: Nobel Peace Laureate Muhammad Yunus on Saturday called on social business practitioners from around the world to translate their ideas into concrete action, saying imagination must be coupled with sustained efforts to build a more inclusive and sustainable future......

item.title: Govt fast-tracking reforms to improve investment climate: minister
item.description: The government is implementing fast-tracked reforms in business registration, licensing, port management and the energy sector to create a more investment-friendly environment, boost industrialisation, generate employment and strengthen Bangladesh’s export competitiveness, commerce minister Khandaker Abdul Muktadir said on Saturday......

item.title: 90 women entrepreneurs receive Amrai TARA training
item.description: BRAC Bank, in partnership with Bangladesh Open Source Network, conducted three cohorts of its flagship women entrepreneurship development programme, Amrai TARA, said a press release......

item.title: Zero tariff on equipment brings hopes in renewable energy
item.description: The BNP government, led by prime minister Tarique Rahman, is prioritising the renewable energy sector to reduce import dependence in power generation, strengthen energy security and ensure environment-friendly energy......

item.title: Trump threatens 100pc tariff on EU countries over digital services tax
item.description: US president Donald Trump on Friday threatened to slap a 100 per cent tariff on European countries that impose a digital services tax, adding that existing trade deals would be scrapped......

item.title: Globalisation isn’t dead, just ‘transformed’
item.description: While the global economy has faced shocks and trade turmoil, globalisation is not dead — it is simply being ‘transformed,’ the International Monetary Fund’s chief economist told AFP Friday in an exclusive interview......

item.title: Should we fear an AI bubble bust?
item.description: Recent swings in tech stocks are reviving fears of an AI bubble — and some experts warn that if it pops, the fallout could be bigger than anything Wall Street has ever seen......

item.title: Venezuela quakes death toll nears 1,000
item.description: Close to seven million people may have been impacted by the twin earthquakes in Venezuela, which have killed nearly 1,000 people and left tens of thousands missing, the United Nations estimated on Saturday....

item.title: Red rocks yield coveted minerals in DR Congo
item.description: In vast warehouses at the Kamoa copper mine in the southeastern Democratic Republic of Congo, mountains of reddish rocks pile up as the machines grind away day and night......

item.title: US allows limited access to Anthropic’s Mythos AI model
item.description: Anthropic said Friday it had received authorisation from the US government to allow a small group of American cybersecurity firms to access its powerful AI model Mythos 5......

item.title: Pabna lawmaker wants school to get back his mother’s name
item.description: The lawmaker for the Pabna-2 constituency, AKM Selim Reza Habib, has sought reinstatement of a school’s name after his mother, which was given to it at the time of its establishment two decades ago at Bera upazila....

item.title: Govt to update disability rights law for better inclusion
item.description: Health state minister M A Muhit has pledged a revision of the Rights and Protection of Person with Disabilities Act to ensure the inclusion of persons with disability across ministries....

item.description: Prime Bank PLC has recently signed an agreement with Bio Build Development Limited to offer exclusive gifts to its customers, said a press release......

item.title: BMU Tk 1,039cr budget focuses on free medicines, cancer care, research
item.description: Bangladesh Medical University has approved a Tk 1,039 crore budget for the 2026–27 financial year with priority on cancer care, free medicines, medical education, research and training....

item.title: Pathao concludes road safety campaign thru cartoons
item.description: Pathao on Saturday concluded its month-long road safety campaign with a special exhibition that brought together cartoons from artists and creative minds across Bangladesh, said a press release......

item.title: Bank Asia partners with Crowne Plaza Dhaka Airport
item.description: Bank Asia PLC has recently signed a strategic partnership agreement with Crowne Plaza Dhaka Airport to provide exclusive lifestyle and hospitality benefits to its cardholders, said a press release......

item.title: JS to pass finance bill tomorrow
item.description: The finance bill 2026 is going to be passed in the Jatiya Sangsad tomorrow with some changes to the original proposals made by the finance minister on June 11....

item.title: Dhaka Bank approves 10pc cash dividend
item.description: Dhaka Bank PLC on June 25 approved a 10 per cent cash dividend for the financial year ended December 31, 2025, said a press release......

item.title: Shippers’ Council holds 2nd board meeting
item.description: Shippers’ Council of Bangladesh on Thursday held its 2nd board meeting for the term 2026 and 2027 at its premises in the capital, said a press release......

item.title: Popular Life wins first prize for anti-drug initiatives
item.description: The Department of Narcotics Control on Friday organised a discussion meeting and award distribution ceremony at Osmani Memorial Auditorium in the capital to mark the International Day Against Drug Abuse and Illicit Trafficking, said a press release......

item.title: Measles deaths cross 700 in Bangladesh
item.description: The death toll from measles has crossed the 700 mark with six new deaths recorded in the latest 24-hour reporting period....

item.title: Govt must curb public service delivery corruption
item.description: AN INCREASE in the prevalence of corruption in access to essential services in 2025, as a Transparency International Bangladesh survey suggests, remains a cause for worry. What is further worrying is that the situation has not improved despite the 2024 political changeover. The survey report, made public on June 25, says that 63.6 per cent of the households surveyed......

item.title: Health sector purchase, upkeep should be transparent
item.description: THE procurement and maintenance of medical and diagnostic equipment for public hospitals have for long been plagued by misgovernance, often at the expense of patients. The minister for health told the parliament on June 25 that 485 X-ray machines in 310 public hospitals and 395 ultrasound machines in 252 public facilities are out of service, limiting access to essential.....

item.title: Welfare, work or enforcement?
item.description: THE budget proposal for the 2026–27 financial year raises an important question: does women’s empowerment mean supporting women only as welfare recipients, or also as workers, earners, and caregivers who need real public support?.....

item.title: Short-video culture and learning
item.description: THE rise of short-video platforms has transformed the cultural landscape in ways few digital innovations have before. Platforms such as TikTok, Facebook and YouTube are now embedded in young people’s daily life. While these platforms offer entertainment, creativity and access to information, they also reshape attention spans, learning habits and cultural values, warranting a careful examination......

item.title: Can Bangladesh become 12th ASEAN member?
item.description: THE foreign policy of Bangladesh is entering a moment in which ASEAN is no longer a distant regional organisation but an increasingly important strategic horizon. Professor Muhammad Yunus had earlier raised Bangladesh’s aspiration for closer engagement with ASEAN, including the possibility of membership. The BNP government also appears interested in closer......

item.title: Spying in South Asia
BRAC Bank is transforming country’s SME landscape through technology
item.description: Bangladesh’s cottage, micro, small and medium enterprises (CMSMEs) have long been the backbone of our economy. They create jobs, drive local industries, encourage innovation and contribute significantly to the country’s economic growth. Yet, for decades, many of these entrepreneurs remained excluded from the formal financial system. Providing them with access......

item.title: Blended finance, technology crucial to turn CMSMEs into growth engine
item.description: Bangladesh must prioritise technological adaptation, digital market access and blended finance to pull cottage, micro, small and medium enterprises out of the low-productivity trap and turn it into a major engine of employment-led economic growth, said Palli Karma-Sahayak Foundation managing director Fazlul Kader Chowdhury......

item.title: Bangladesh’s CMSMEs expand 50pc in a decade
item.description: Bangladesh’s cottage, micro, small and medium enterprises have expanded over the past decade, emerging as the country’s largest source of business establishments and employment......

item.title: The new era of MSME growth
item.description: Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME) have firmly established themselves as the driving force behind Bangladesh’s economic narrative. Generating a massive chunk of employment nationwide, these resilient businesses keep manufacturing, services, agriculture, exports, and local markets humming. Yet, for this sector to achieve truly sustainable growth......

item.title: Technology key to unlocking SME potential
item.description: Bangladesh’s small and medium enterprise sector must rapidly adopt digital technologies, artificial intelligence and Fourth Industrial Revolution tools to become more productive, competitive and capable of creating jobs on a scale, SME Foundation managing director Anwar Hossain Chowdhury said......

item.title: Data gap holding back Bangladesh SMEs
item.description: Bangladesh’s 11.7 million small and medium enterprises (SMEs) continue to operate far below their potential largely because of the absence of reliable data systems linking businesses with finance, markets and investment opportunities, a gap that experts say is holding back productivity, formalisation and economic growth......

item.title: Croatia battle past Ghana to sew up Last 32 spot
item.description: Croatia beat Ghana 2-1 to claim second spot in Group L on Saturday to secure their passage to the last 32 of the World Cup....

item.title: Bellingham, Kane score as England beat Panama to reach last 32
item.description: Jude Bellingham and Harry Kane scored second-half goals as England got the job done against Panama at the World Cup on Saturday, a 2-0 victory at the MetLife Stadium taking Thomas Tuchel's team into the last 32 as Group L winners....

item.title: 'It was a dream': Egypt revel in historic World Cup moment
item.description:
For Karim Ramadan, watching from a buzzing Cairo cafe before sunrise, the moment felt almost unreal as Egypt progressed from the group stage of a World Cup for the first time in their history on Saturday.
...

item.title: World Cup star Gakpo requests privacy after death of unborn son
item.description: Netherlands forward Cody Gakpo has asked for ‘privacy and space’ following the loss of his unborn son, which was announced by his partner Noa van der Bij....

item.title: Williams hits out at Uruguay over injury
item.description:
Spain winger Nico Williams said suffering a latest injury setback was ‘one of the worst days of his life’ as he criticised Uruguay for lashing out in a 1-0 defeat that ended their World Cup.
...

item.title: 'Why not?': Cape Verde eye seismic World Cup shock against Argentina
item.description: Cape Verde say they have the quality, the heart and the global football family behind them as they size up a seismic World Cup shock against Lionel Messi's Argentina....

item.description: Lebanon, Israel and have signed an agreement hoping to end hostilities, but experts warn that it does not guarantee peace and its implementation depends on Hezbollah and Iran......

Generating a massive chunk of employment nationwide, these resilient businesses keep manufacturing, services, agriculture, exports, and local markets humming. Yet, for this sector to achieve truly sustainable growth......

item.description: The management information systems department at East West University has organised a discussion titled Artificial Intelligence in Industry: Transforming Education and the Future Workplace at the Nousher Ali Lecture Gallery of the university in Dhaka recently....GeographicReference

item.description: Steel manufacturer KSRM on Saturday donated advanced medical equipment worth nearly Tk 2 crore to Chattogram Medical College Hospital, aiming to strengthen the hospital’s capacity to provide critical healthcare services and improve treatment for thousands of patients....

item.title: Govt to formulate budget ignoring uprising spirit: speakers
item.description: Speaking at a discussion meeting on the national budget of financial year 2026-27 in Dhaka on Saturday, politicians, academics and professionals stated that the Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government was formulating a budget that ignores the spirit of the 2024 July Uprising....

item.description: An accused escaped injuring two police officers during a drive at Gendukuri under Hatibandha in Lalmonirhat on Friday night....

item.description: The police recovered the body of a youth with disabilities from the Kali River under Kumarkhali in Kushtia on Saturday morning....

item.description: Commerce minister Khandaker Abdul Muktadir on Saturday said that if Chinese investment in Bangladesh increases, the existing trade deficit between the two countries will come down significantly....

item.title: ‘Robber’ killed in Sundarban gunfight
item.description: An alleged robber was killed and two others, including chief of the gang named ‘Dulabhai Bahini’, were arrested following a gunfight with the Bangladesh Coast Guard in the Sundarban at Koyra in Khulna on Saturday....

item.description: Despite a major anti-crime crackdown that led to dozens of arrests and the recovery of firearms last year, armed gangs continue to operate across the Padma River chars, where at least seven people have been killed over the past nine months....

item.description: The police on Saturday night arrested two people with 4,000 counterfeit Tk 1,000 notes in a drive at Motalib Plaza in ter capital......

item.description: Rainfall is likely to continue across the country over the next five days as the monsoon remains fairly active over Bangladesh, according to the Bangladesh Meteorological Department......

item.description: Dhaka and Beijing have agreed to launch a feasibility study of the proposed Teesta River Comprehensive Management and Restoration Project inside Bangladesh soon, involving experts from both the countries....

item.description: Apple said Thursday it was raising prices for its MacBook computers, iPad tablets and other products, citing spiralling memory and storage costs sparked by the rise of artificial intelligence......

item.description: Dhaka Metropolitan Police arrested three activists of Awami League( now facing ban on its activities) and its affiliated organisations from different parts of the capital in 24 hours till Saturday morning......

item.description: Tens of millions face a weekend of extreme temperatures in Europe as a deadly heatwave moves eastwards, with German forecasters warning that more records could be broken and eastern countries issuing a slew of red alerts for the coming days......

item.description: South Korea said it had sent up fighter jets as a precaution after more than 10 Chinese and Russian military aircraft entered its air defence zone on Saturday......

item.description: US health authorities on Friday activated the highest level of response to the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo, while announcing the shipment of experimental treatments to the region......

item.description: A pilot was killed when his small aircraft crashed into Beijing’s tallest building, with 13 people also injured, Chinese authorities said on Saturday without elaborating on the cause of the rare incident......

item.title: Tunisia upholds sentence against jailed journalist
item.description: A Tunisian appeals court upheld the one-year prison sentence of independent journalist Zied el-Heni on Friday after he criticised judges for wrongly convicting others in his profession......

item.description: Burkina Faso’s ruling junta on Friday severed diplomatic ties with former colonial ruler France, accusing Paris of persistently acting against its interests......

item.title: Australia to double fine for teen social media ban
item.description: Australia will double the financial penalty on platforms flouting its world-leading social media ban for under-16s to stem widespread evasion of the restrictions, the government said on Saturday......

item.description: Dhaka, the capital of Bangladesh, ranked 32nd among the world’s most polluted cities on Saturday morning, with an Air Quality Index score of 67......

item.description: Indonesian police have arrested dozens of protesters following a rally against president Prabowo Subianto’s policies in the country’s second-largest city, a rights group said on Saturday......

item.title: UK to expand refugee sponsorship schemes
item.description: The UK interior ministry has announced it will create new legal routes for asylum seekers, including allowing community organisations to sponsor refugees based on a similar system in Canada......

item.title: Sanctioned Cuban military group cedes key assets
item.description: Cuba’s sanctioned military conglomerate GAESA, which controls much of the island’s economy, has spun off its port management business seeking to unlock stalled foreign supplies, several industry sources told AFP Friday......

item.description: A college student was stabbed to death allegedly by his rivals over previous enmity at Borobhag Eastpara village in Alfadanga upazila of Faridpur on Friday evening......

item.description: The Central African Republic has declared a cholera outbreak after confirming 24 deaths and 197 cases near the capital Bangui, according to the health minister......

item.description: South Africa coach Hugo Broos smiles a lot and snarls a little as he prepares for the biggest match of his 38-year career as a tactician....

item.description: Experts and activists at a national symposium on Saturday said that women’s empowerment could not be achieved without ensuring their meaningful political participation, representation and leadership....

item.description: Japan can beat Brazil in the World Cup last 32 by embracing an ‘underdog mentality’, Monaco striker Takumi Minamino said....

item.description: United States president Donald Trump on Friday unveiled a rendering of a new limited-edition US passport to mark the country’s 250th anniversary of independence — featuring his stern-faced likeness......

item.description: A 6.1-magnitude earthquake hit Afghanistan on Saturday, the United States Geological Survey said, with AFP journalists in the capital Kabul and elsewhere reporting strong shaking......

item.description: England and Portugal will aim to finish on a high as the World Cup group stages draw to a close on Saturday with the final places in the knockout rounds up for grabs....

item.description: A mental health awareness programme focusing on self-confidence, resilience and emotional wellbeing was held at the University of Dhaka on Saturday that brought together students to discuss the importance of mental health in academic and personal life......GeographicReference

item.description: Bangladesh Nationalist Party lawmaker Gayeshwar Chandra Roy on Saturday said the proposed national budget could be implemented successfully only if the government took effective steps to curb corruption and reform public administration......

item.description: Norway coach Stale Solbakken defended his decision to leave out star duo Erling Haaland and Martin Odegaard in Friday's 4-1 World Cup defeat against France, saying it was a ‘no-brainer’....

item.description: Three members of a family were stabbed by their rivals in the capital’s Mohakhali area under Banani police station allegedly due to a dispute over an unpaid debt......
